Becoming

✦ A Memoir · The Art of Evolving

BecomingHer

"I didn't wake up one day and suddenly became her. In fact, for the longest time, I wasn't even sure who she was supposed to be."

❧
Get Your Copy Read More
✦ The Book

What is Becoming Her?

There is a version of you that exists somewhere in the future — composed, certain, whole. This is the story of what it takes to find her. Not in a single awakening, but in the quiet collapse and slow rebuilding that nobody warns you about.

Becoming Her is a memoir that sits at the intersection of identity, loss, and the terrifying freedom of starting over. Written in the voice of someone who refused to let her falling-apart be the end of the story.

"She didn't find herself. She built herself, piece by deliberate piece, from the rubble of everything she thought she was."

— Becoming Her

This is not a book about having it all figured out. It is a book about what happens when you don't — and how that might be exactly where the becoming begins.

Inside the Memoir

  • i.
    The UnravelingOn the particular grief of losing the version of yourself you thought was permanent
  • ii.
    The In-BetweenThe strange, liminal season of not knowing who you are becoming
  • iii.
    Learning to StayHow she learned to sit with herself — even when herself was a difficult companion
  • iv.
    The RebuildingChoosing, with intention, every brick of the woman she was becoming
  • v.
    Her, FinallyNot an arrival — but the first morning she recognised her own reflection

On the Name

My name is Prachi. And Prayora is not a pseudonym — it is a prayer.

I chose it because it is a modification of the word prayer — a name for the version of me that does not yet fully exist, but is quietly, persistently being built. Prayora is who I am becoming. Prachi is who I am.

This space lives in the distance between those two names — and everything that happens in that in-between.

✦ The Author

About Prachi

She reads like it keeps her alive, and writes like she is afraid she will forget the things that matter most. She is, by her own admission, someone who does not love easily — but when she does, she holds on deeply.

A writer and thinker navigating the particular chaos of becoming, she lives a parallel life in words — in the margins of notebooks, in late-night poetry drafts, in a memoir she started writing because there was no other way to survive the version of herself that was falling apart.

Prayora is her space for becoming. For the writing that doesn't fit neatly anywhere else. For the slow, honest work of figuring out who you are when no one is watching — and choosing, anyway, to become her.

Reader Writer Double Leo Dark Romance Slow Burn Selective Guarded Poet
✦ From the Pages

Fragments & Verses

Poetry

"I am not what happened to me. I am what I chose to become from the pieces of it — the ones I kept, and the ones I quietly buried."

Becoming Her — Chapter II
Prose

"She had this way of holding herself — like she was both the wound and the one who decided not to let it win. That, I think, was the most beautiful thing about her."

Becoming Her — Chapter IV
Reflection

"Guarded is not the same as cold. It is the particular warmth of someone who has learned, carefully, who deserves to be let in."

Guarded — Instagram, 2024
